[[JA:佐麻多度神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11385140}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Samatado Shrine |画像=[[File:佐麻多度神社 (八尾市)01.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Yamahata 340 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Samatado Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}) |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Samatado
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Samatado Shrine''' (さまたどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === ;Main deity *Samatado Ōkami (さまたどおおかみ) :Samatado Ōkami is the ubusuna deity of Yamahata village, but details are unclear because historical materials on Samatado Ōkami and related ancient clans are unknown[http://www.y-tohara.com/naka-samatado.html Shikinaisha of Kawachi / Samatado Shrine (Yao)] accessed 2019-02-17. The '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}' records it as "Sawa-me deity" and "Haniyasu deity". === History === Founding year and original deity are unknown, but it is said to have been founded in the Yamato period (600s CE). The original site was about 100 m higher, within temple lands of Byakka Kannon-in at Taniguchi Tenjinyama on the slope of {{ill|lt=Takayasuyama|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140|1=Mount Takayasu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} Takayasu District. With the 1872 (Meiji 5) separation of Shinto and Buddhism decree, the temple was abolished and Samatado Shrine was ranked as village shrine. In April of the same year, it merged with Yamahata Shrine. In June 1899 (Meiji 32), it moved to the current site because visiting had been inconvenient. === Precinct === On the south side of the precinct beside the torii are four "{{ill|lt=chikaraishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}}" stones weighing about 165 kg each. They were used in strength contests once enjoyed by young men of Yamahata village[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=67 Samatado Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System] accessed 2019-02-17. Also, an unglazed {{ill|lt=ceramic coffin|Q18337447|WD=Q18337447|1=Ceramic Coffins}} was excavated around the precinct area in the past, and is now kept at {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. 佐麻多度神社 (八尾市)02.JPG|Yamahata Shrine (left) and Yamahata Tenmangū (right), end shrines in precinct 佐麻多度神社 (八尾市)03.JPG|Chikaraishi in the precinct === Precinct shrines === * Yamahata Shrine :The sessha Yamahata Shrine is identified as the former Shikinaisha of Takayasu District, "Kasugato-sha ni imasu Miko Shrine." Locally, it is also called "Takaza Myōjin." * Yamahata Tenmangū :The Tenmangū honden was moved from the shrine building of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsufukumi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} that had once been merged into {{ill|lt=Tamaso Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in 1907 (Meiji 40)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=134 Tsufukumi Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System] accessed 2019-02-17. === Local information === ;Location * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Yamahata 340 ;Transportation * Nearest station: about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|1=Takayasu}} * {{ill|lt=Chikaraishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Samatado-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_19_yao_samatado.html Samatado Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samatado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samatado Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]

Torii and haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Yamahata 340 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Samatado Ōkami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Autumn festival ({{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}) |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Samatado
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Samatado Shrine''' (さまたどじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === ;Main deity *Samatado Ōkami (さまたどおおかみ) :Samatado Ōkami is the ubusuna deity of Yamahata village, but details are unclear because historical materials on Samatado Ōkami and related ancient clans are unknown[http://www.y-tohara.com/naka-samatado.html Shikinaisha of Kawachi / Samatado Shrine (Yao)] accessed 2019-02-17. The '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}' records it as "Sawa-me deity" and "Haniyasu deity". === History === Founding year and original deity are unknown, but it is said to have been founded in the Yamato period (600s CE). The original site was about 100 m higher, within temple lands of Byakka Kannon-in at Taniguchi Tenjinyama on the slope of {{ill|lt=Takayasuyama|Q11669140|WD=Q11669140|1=Mount Takayasu}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as a small shrine in {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}} Takayasu District. With the 1872 (Meiji 5) separation of Shinto and Buddhism decree, the temple was abolished and Samatado Shrine was ranked as village shrine. In April of the same year, it merged with Yamahata Shrine. In June 1899 (Meiji 32), it moved to the current site because visiting had been inconvenient. === Precinct === On the south side of the precinct beside the torii are four "{{ill|lt=chikaraishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}}" stones weighing about 165 kg each. They were used in strength contests once enjoyed by young men of Yamahata village[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=67 Samatado Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System] accessed 2019-02-17. Also, an unglazed {{ill|lt=ceramic coffin|Q18337447|WD=Q18337447|1=Ceramic Coffins}} was excavated around the precinct area in the past, and is now kept at {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. 佐麻多度神社 (八尾市)02.JPG|Yamahata Shrine (left) and Yamahata Tenmangū (right), end shrines in precinct 佐麻多度神社 (八尾市)03.JPG|Chikaraishi in the precinct === Precinct shrines === * Yamahata Shrine :The sessha Yamahata Shrine is identified as the former Shikinaisha of Takayasu District, "Kasugato-sha ni imasu Miko Shrine." Locally, it is also called "Takaza Myōjin." * Yamahata Tenmangū :The Tenmangū honden was moved from the shrine building of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsufukumi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} that had once been merged into {{ill|lt=Tamaso Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in 1907 (Meiji 40)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=134 Tsufukumi Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System] accessed 2019-02-17. === Local information === ;Location * {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Yamahata 340 ;Transportation * Nearest station: about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Notes === === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Takayasu (Yao)|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|1=Takayasu}} * {{ill|lt=Chikaraishi|Q1660393|WD=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Samatado-jinja}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_19_yao_samatado.html Samatado Shrine - Osaka Prefectural Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Samatado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Samatado Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]
